examples.title: Azure Blob Storage - File Upload and Download
examples.description: Demonstrates basic Azure Blob Storage operations using the Azure SDK - uploading, listing, and downloading blobs from a container.
examples.code:
from azure.storage.blob import BlobServiceClient, BlobClient, ContainerClient
import os

# Connection string from Azure Portal > Storage Account > Access keys
CONNECTION_STRING = "your_azure_storage_connection_string"
CONTAINER_NAME = "my-container"
LOCAL_FILE_PATH = "sample.txt"
BLOB_NAME = "sample.txt"

# Create the BlobServiceClient
blob_service_client = BlobServiceClient.from_connection_string(CONNECTION_STRING)

# Get container client (creates container if not exists)
container_client = blob_service_client.get_container_client(CONTAINER_NAME)
if not container_client.exists():
    container_client = blob_service_client.create_container(CONTAINER_NAME)

# Upload a file to Blob Storage
def upload_blob(file_path, blob_name):
    blob_client = blob_service_client.get_blob_client(container=CONTAINER_NAME, blob=blob_name)
    with open(file_path, "rb") as data:
        blob_client.upload_blob(data, overwrite=True)
    print(f"Uploaded: {blob_name}")

# List all blobs in container
def list_blobs():
    blobs = container_client.list_blobs()
    print("Blobs in container:")
    for blob in blobs:
        print(f"  - {blob.name}")

# Download a blob
def download_blob(blob_name, download_path):
    blob_client = blob_service_client.get_blob_client(container=CONTAINER_NAME, blob=blob_name)
    with open(download_path, "wb") as download_file:
        download_file.write(blob_client.download_blob().readall())
    print(f"Downloaded: {blob_name}")

# Main execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create sample file
    with open(LOCAL_FILE_PATH, "w") as f:
        f.write("Hello from Azure Blob Storage!")
    
    upload_blob(LOCAL_FILE_PATH, BLOB_NAME)
    list_blobs()
    download_blob(BLOB_NAME, "downloaded.txt")
    print("Azure Blob Storage operations completed!")
examples.explanation: 1. Import Azure Blob Storage SDK modules. 2. Define connection string (from Azure Portal). 3. Create BlobServiceClient for connection. 4. Create/get container using ContainerClient. 5. Upload file using BlobClient.upload_blob(). 6. List all blobs using container_client.list_blobs(). 7. Download blob using blob_client.download_blob().
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for listing, O(1) for upload/download

examples.title: Azure Functions - HTTP Trigger with Queue Output
examples.description: Creates an Azure Function with HTTP trigger that processes input and writes results to an Azure Queue Storage.
examples.explanation: 1. Import Azure Functions SDK. 2. Create FunctionApp instance. 3. Define HTTP trigger route with @app.route decorator. 4. Add queue output binding with @app.queue_output. 5. Extract JSON body from HTTP request. 6. Validate 'name' parameter exists. 7. If valid, set queue message and return success JSON response. 8. If invalid, return error response with status 400. 9. The function.json is auto-generated by decorators.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - constant time for request processing

examples.title: Azure Cognitive Services - Text Analytics for Sentiment Analysis
examples.description: Demonstrates Azure Cognitive Services Text Analytics API to perform sentiment analysis on text documents, including language detection and key phrase extraction.
examples.code:
from azure.ai.textanalytics import TextAnalyticsClient
from azure.core.credentials import AzureKeyCredential
import os

# Azure Cognitive Services credentials
ENDPOINT = "https://your-resource.cognitiveservices.azure.com/"
API_KEY = "your_text_analytics_api_key"

# Authenticate client
def authenticate_client():
    credential = AzureKeyCredential(API_KEY)
    text_analytics_client = TextAnalyticsClient(
        endpoint=ENDPOINT, 
        credential=credential
    )
    return text_analytics_client

# Sentiment Analysis
def sentiment_analysis(client, documents):
    result = client.analyze_sentiment(documents, show_opinion_mining=True)
    
    print("\n=== SENTIMENT ANALYSIS ===")
    for doc in result:
        print(f"Document: {doc.id}")
        print(f"  Sentiment: {doc.sentiment}")
        print(f"  Confidence scores: positive={doc.confidence_scores.positive:.2f}, "
              f"neutral={doc.confidence_scores.neutral:.2f}, "
              f"negative={doc.confidence_scores.negative:.2f}")

# Language Detection
def language_detection(client, documents):
    result = client.detect_language(documents)
    
    print("\n=== LANGUAGE DETECTION ===")
    for doc in result:
        print(f"Document: {doc.id}")
        print(f"  Primary language: {doc.primary_language.name} "
              f"(ISO: {doc.primary_language.iso6391_name})")

# Key Phrase Extraction
def key_phrase_extraction(client, documents):
    result = client.extract_key_phrases(documents)
    
    print("\n=== KEY PHRASE EXTRACTION ===")
    for doc in result:
        print(f"Document: {doc.id}")
        print(f"  Key phrases: {', '.join(doc.key_phrases)}")

# Entity Recognition
def entity_recognition(client, documents):
    result = client.recognize_entities(documents)
    
    print("\n=== ENTITY RECOGNITION ===")
    for doc in result:
        print(f"Document: {doc.id}")
        for entity in doc.entities:
            print(f"  - {entity.text}: {entity.category} (confidence: {entity.confidence_score:.2f})")

# Main execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
    documents = [
        {"id": "1", "language": "en", "text": "Azure Cognitive Services is amazing! The sentiment analysis API is very powerful and easy to use."},
        {"id": "2", "language": "en", "text": "I had a terrible experience with the service. The documentation is confusing and the API response times are slow."},
        {"id": "3", "language": "es", "text": "Me encanta usar Microsoft Azure para proyectos de aprendizaje automático."}
    ]
    
    client = authenticate_client()
    
    # Run all analyses
    language_detection(client, documents)
    sentiment_analysis(client, documents)
    key_phrase_extraction(client, documents)
    entity_recognition(client, documents)
    
    print("\nAzure Cognitive Services Text Analytics completed!")
examples.explanation: 1. Import Azure Text Analytics SDK and credentials. 2. Set endpoint and API key from Azure Portal. 3. Authenticate using AzureKeyCredential. 4. Create TextAnalyticsClient. 5. Define documents list with id, language, and text. 6. Call analyze_sentiment() for sentiment analysis (positive/neutral/negative). 7. Call detect_language() for language detection. 8. Call extract_key_phrases() to find important terms. 9. Call recognize_entities() to identify named entities (persons, organizations, locations).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of documents
